reason: The statement does not apply to this text because it depicts Yahweh changing his mind in response to the actions of the Israelites. Initially, Yahweh expresses anger and intention to consume the people, but after Moses intercedes and pleads on behalf of the people, Yahweh relents and chooses not to bring the disaster he had threatened. This shows a change in Yahweh's initial decision, indicating a shift in his mindset based on the situation at hand.
